observations.recommendation:
2.8 We recommended that the Management:
2.8.1 Advise the Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfers.
2.8.2 Initiate the request for a write-off of the Inter-agency Receivables that remain non-moving for almost ten (10) years or more in the books, in accordance with COA Circular 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We have recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reclassify the transactions totaling to P4,061,247.67 into their appropriate accounts; and

Review the basis in recording the transactions totaling P291,395.05 and effect the necessary correcting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified and effected the necessary journal entries to correct the balance of the Due to LGUs for transactions totaling P4,352,642.72

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Transfer from General Fund to Trust Fund the unspent DRRMF totaling P9,837,951.85 and record the following adjusting entries:

Ensure the transfer at year-end the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund and comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, in Journal Entry Vouchers Nos. 100-09-0083 and 100-09-0084, effected the correcting entries to appropriately record the transfer of the unspent DRRMF. Further, the Management, in compliance with the recommendations, transferred from the GF the total unspent DRRMF of P9,837,951.85 to Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted programs, projects and activities identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction: The Management reprogrammed its accumulated unexpended LDRRMF. The utilization rate of the fund for CY 2024 increased to 59 percent,
observations.reason: The Management did not optimally utilize the fund for CY 2024 due to the funding by outside sources of its proposed PPAs in its LDRRM Investment Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

A. The BAC require the submission of Omnibus Sworn Statement on all procurements made through Negotiated Procurement-Small Value Procurement with ABCs above P50,000.00 pursuant to the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184; and

B. The Municipal Accountant ensure compliance with the documentary requirements before payment in order to establish validity of the procurements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management Implemented the Audit Recommendations by submitting Omnibus Sworn Statement of bidders/suppliers.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

A.	Consolidate requisitions for procurement of goods and services from each department/office into a single Annual Procurement Plan as basis in planning the procurement of the Municipality for the entire year pursuant to the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184;

B.	Require the BAC to meticulously plan the procurement of the Municipality and deliberate properly on the appropriate mode of procurement to avoid resorting to splitting of contracts; and

C.	Submit justification on the splitting of requisitions and contracts for the above projects.
observations.managementAction: The management already stopped the practice of splitting of contracts and requisitions. They already  consolidate all requisitions based on the Annual Procurement Plan.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

A.	Submit justification on why it proceeded with the repair of the vehicle despite the assessment made by the GSIS that the vehicle is beyond economic repair; and

B.	Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to ensure that repairs for vehicles and equipment shall not be allowed if the vehicle is beyond economic repair, otherwise the cost of repairs shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The repairs were made to vehicles or equipment that is beyond economic repair for the year. The Management request for disposal of unserviceable and beyond repair vehicles and equipment.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, submit the lacking supporting documents and strictly comply with the documentary requirements for payment of honoraria provided in COA Circular 2023-004.
observations.managementAction: The management submitted the necessary documents to support payment of Honoraria.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify the transactions totaling to P4,061,247.67 into their appropriate accounts; and
Review the basis in recording the transactions totaling P291,395.05 and effect the necessary correcting entries.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Transfer from General Fund to Trust Fund the unspent DRRMF totaling P9,837,951.85 and record the following adjusting entries:
General Fund
Trust Fund
Ensure the transfer at year-end the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund and comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted programs, projects and activities identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.

We recommended the following:
The BAC require the submission of Omnibus Sworn Statement on all procurements made through Negotiated Procurement-Small Value Procurement with ABCs above P50,000.00 pursuant to the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184; and
The Municipal Accountant ensure compliance with the documentary requirements before payment in order to establish validity of the procurements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Consolidate requisitions for procurement of goods and services from each department/office into a single Annual Procurement Plan as basis in planning the procurement of the Municipality for the entire year pursuant to the Revised IRR of Republic Act No. 9184;
Require the BAC to meticulously plan the procurement of the Municipality and deliberate properly on the appropriate mode of procurement to avoid resorting to splitting of contracts; and
Submit justification on the splitting of requisitions and contracts for the above projects.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification on why it proceeded with the repair of the vehicle despite the assessment made by the GSIS that the vehicle is beyond economic repair; and
Direct the GSO and Municipal Accountant to ensure that repairs for vehicles and equipment shall not be allowed if the vehicle is beyond economic repair, otherwise the cost of repairs shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, submit the lacking supporting documents and strictly comply with the documentary requirements for payment of honoraria provided in COA Circular 2023-004.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Immediately record the above Infrastructure Assets under the Property Plant and Equipment account and recognize its accompanying Depreciation. 

Record all Infrastructure Assets upon completion of the project.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant effected the correcting journal entries to record the completed Infrastructure Assets under the Property Plant and Equipment account and recognized its accompanying Depreciation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Accountant and GSO Designate to reconcile the reports submitted and correct the Property Plant and Equipment account presented in the Financials Statements.

The GSO Designate and Inventory Committee to include physical count of land and infrastructure assets in the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the GSO reconciled the balances of the PPE in the RPCPPE and Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

The Management to allot a budget under the General Fund for the payment of Job Order and other administrative expenses related to the operations and implementation of 20% Development Fund.

The Accountant and Budget Officer to return the remaining fund of the Due to LGUs-Engineering Fee account to the 20% Development Fund.

The MPDO and ME to stop the charging 3% Engineering Fee to the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management stopped the practice of charging three percent (3%) Engineering Fee to the projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: The Management did not return the fund to the 20% Development Fund because it was not able to prepare a supplemental budget and investment plan for the available fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation to fast track the implementation of its 20% development fund projects. For CY 2023, the Municipality utilized 88.62 percent or P42,077,852.77 of its total 20% development fund of P47,481,481.68.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted projects identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.
observations.managementAction:
Reiterated in Finding No. 3, Part II of this report.

observations.reason: The Management did not optimally utilize the fund for CY 2023 due to the funding by outside sources of its proposed PPAs in its LDRRM Investment Plan.

The Management to allot a budget under the General Fund for the payment of Job Order and other administrative expenses related to the operations and implementation of 20% Development Fund.

observations.managementAction: We recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted projects identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.

We have recommended, pursuant to Sections 114 and 121 of the New Government Accounting Systems for LGUs, Volume I, that the Management set up the proper safeguards on various supplies, drugs and medicines by requiring:

The General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards for inventories received, to issue Requisition and Issue Slip for the issuance of inventories and prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the required Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued.
The Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each specific type of Inventory.

The periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. The physical count of unissued inventory items by type shall be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories.
observations.managementAction: Management initially recognized supplies as Inventories but expensed the same at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management:

Require the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the expired portion of Insurance on a monthly basis.

Make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the Financial Statements as of a given date.

Advise the Accountant to strictly adhere to existing applicable accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management immediately return to the source NGAs the idle funds aggregating to ₱1,425,765.34 pursuant to Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012, on the submission, and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies.
observations.managementAction: Management returned some amount to the source agencies and the Municipality sent letter to other source agencies to request the use of the fund for other related projects.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Majority of the unimplemented projects were implemented for CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management closely monitor the activities in the LDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely implemented in addressing projected climate risk and enhancing disaster capabilities at the LGU level.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality reprogrammed the LDRRM Fund and implemented the same during CY 2022

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management provide budgetary support to the MDRRM Office under the General Fund and refrain from using the LDRRMF for non-priority programs, projects, and activities that are not related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation, and recovery.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Management submit a justification on the payment of Honoraria to the assigned Municipal Local Government Operations Officer, otherwise, discontinue the payment of honoraria and other allowances without legal basis.
observations.managementAction: Management discontinued the payment of MLGOO Honoraria.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, pursuant to Sections 114 and 121 of the New Government Accounting Systems for LGUs, Volume I, that the Management set up the proper safeguards on various supplies, drugs and medicines by requiring:
The General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards for inventories received, to issue Requisition and Issue Slip for the issuance of inventories and prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the required Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued.
The Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each specific type of Inventory.
The periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. The physical count of unissued inventory items by type shall be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to recognize the expired portion of Insurance on a monthly basis.
Make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the Financial Statements as of a given date.
Advise the Accountant to strictly adhere to existing applicable accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management immediately return to the source NGAs the idle funds aggregating to ₱1,425,765.34 pursuant to Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012, on the submission, and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies.

We recommended that the management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Management closely monitor the activities in the LDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely implemented in addressing projected climate risk and enhancing disaster capabilities at the LGU level.

We recommended that the Management provide budgetary support to the MDRRM Office under the General Fund and refrain from using the LDRRMF for non-priority programs, projects, and activities that are not related to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation, and recovery.

We recommended that the Management submit a justification on the payment of Honoraria to the assigned Municipal Local Government Operations Officer, otherwise, discontinue the payment of honoraria and other allowances without legal basis.

We recommended, pursuant to Sections 182, 187 and 189 Volume 1 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to set up the proper safeguards on various supplies, drugs and medicines that:

a. The General Services Officer maintain stock cards for inventories received, issue Requisition and Issue Slip for the issuance of inventories and prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the required Report of Materials and Supplies Issued five days after the end of the month.

b. The Municipal Accountant maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each specific type of Inventories.

c. The Municipal Mayor require periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. The physical count of unissued inventory items by type shall be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).
observations.managementAction: Management initially recognized supplies as Inventories but expensed the same at the end of the year.
Management implemented the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management request for write-off the dormant and long outstanding accounts that remained for more than ten (10) years in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management requested for the write-off of the dormant and long outstanding accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to notify and direct all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO), for the receipt and inspection of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department necessary records on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended, pursuant to Sections 182, 187 and 189 Volume 1 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs to set up the proper safeguards on various supplies, drugs and medicines that:
The General Services Officer maintain stock cards for inventories received, issue Requisition and Issue Slip for the issuance of inventories and prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the required Report of Materials and Supplies Issued five days after the end of the month.
The Municipal Accountant maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each specific type of Inventories.
The Municipal Mayor require periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials. The physical count of unissued inventory items by type shall be conducted at least once a year and reported in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

We recommended that Management request for write-off the dormant and long outstanding accounts that remained for more than ten (10) years in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
3.6. Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation.

We recommended management to notify and direct all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO), for the receipt and inspection of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department or end-user.  The GSO shall likewise furnish the accounting department the necessary records on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended management, thru the Accounting Office, to reclassify the retention money and bid security as Due to LGU accounts to Guaranty/Deposits Payable account, in their respective fund, to conform with the Revised Local Government Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-09 dated December 1, 2015, for fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management has reclassified the accounts to Guaranty/Deposits Payable

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a.	GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the municipality’s road network system;

b.	Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects; and thereafter,

c.	Municipal Accountant to record amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.
observations.managementAction: Management have not recorded the Local Road Network for 2012 and prior years in the books of accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management return immediately to the source NGAs the idle funds aggregating P1,624,903.64 pursuant to Section 4 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission, and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies (NGAs) to make these funds available for appropriation to other priority programs, projects and activities of the government.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required liquidation reports and remitted the unexpended amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the contractor (Construction of NCDC Building) to replace/rectify the item of work (Item 7.Windows- Security Grills), or to refund the deficiency amounting to P33,073.66.   The deficiency can also be deducted from any claim of the contractor upon notice of the municipality to the contractor.  A Notice of Disallowance shall be issued if no action was made by the contractor and/or management within fifteen days (15) upon receipt hereof.
observations.managementAction: Management deducted the deficiency from the claim of the contractor

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to require the contractor (Construction of Local Bridge), to refund the deficiency amounting to P5,073.66, The deficiency can also be deducted from any claim of the contractor upon notice of the municipality to the contractor.  A Notice of Disallowance shall be issued if no action was made by the contractor and/or management within fifteen days (15) upon receipt hereof.
observations.managementAction: The contractor refunded the deficiency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the Statement of Work Accomplished at the expiry date of the contract on the Improvement of Multi-Purpose Building project in Barangay Centro I, for proper evaluation and for the computation of liquidated damages (LD) per Annex “E” 8.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R. A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the report and deducted the amount of liquidated damages from the claim of the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and fully deliver on time these benefits to the community. 

Likewise, the concerned department is advised to monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan and closely coordinate with other departments to fully achieve the desired goals and benefits as planned.
observations.managementAction: Unimplemented programs and projects under 20% DF were realigned for COVID 19 activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management enhance the Policy Guidelines in hiring contract of service employees by setting standards or parameters to clearly determine the reasonable number of contract of service personnel to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower, work duplication and unnecessary expenditures for wages.
observations.managementAction: Management already amended their guidelines in the hiring of COS personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management formulate Policy Guidelines in hiring contract of service employees, taking into consideration the necessity as to the reasonable number of contract of service employees to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower, work duplication and unnecessary expenditures for wages.

Ensure that the policy guidelines formulated would not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the municipality but promote optimum utilization of financial resources.
observations.managementAction: The management has formulated a Policy Guidelines in the hiring of COS but  the policy guidelines does not clearly indicates total required or ideal number of COS personnel to be hired or needed during the year.
observations.reason: Reiterated on Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management, thru the Accounting Office, to reclassify the retention money and bid security as Due to LGU accounts to Guaranty/Deposits Payable account, in their respective fund, to conform with the Revised Local Government Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-09 dated December 1, 2015, for fair presentation of financial statements.

a. 	GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the municipality’s road network system;
b.	Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects; and thereafter,
c.	Municipal Accountant to record amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.

Require the contractor (Construction of NCDC Building) to replace/rectify the item of work (Item 7.Windows- Security Grills), or to refund the deficiency amounting to P33,073.66.   The deficiency can also be deducted from any claim of the contractor upon notice of the municipality to the contractor.  A Notice of Disallowance shall be issued if no action was made by the contractor and/or management within fifteen days (15) upon receipt hereof.

Require the contractor (Construction of Local Bridge), to refund the deficiency amounting to P5,073.66, The deficiency can also be deducted from any claim of the contractor upon notice of the municipality to the contractor.  A Notice of Disallowance shall be issued if no action was made by the contractor and/or management within fifteen days (15) upon receipt hereof.

We recommended that management submit the Statement of Work Accomplished at the expiry date of the contract on the Improvement of Multi-Purpose Building project in Barangay Centro I, for proper evaluation and for the computation of liquidated damages (LD) per Annex “E” 8.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R. A. 9184.

We recommended that Management fast track the completion of all on-going and unimplemented projects funded under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and fully deliver on time these benefits to the community.

Likewise, the concerned department is advised to monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan and closely coordinate with other departments to fully achieve the desired goals and benefits as planned.

We recommended that Management enhance the Policy Guidelines in hiring contract of service employees by setting standards or parameters to clearly determine the reasonable number of contract of service personnel to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower, work duplication and unnecessary expenditures for wages.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. 	GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the municipality’s road network system;

b.	Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects; and thereafter,

c.	Municipal Accountant to record amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.
observations.managementAction: The management did not record 100% of the Local Road Network in the books of accounts
observations.reason: Reiterated on Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, thru the Accounting Office, reclassify the Markets account to Investment Property, Building account to conform with IPSAS 16-Investment Property, for fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation to reclassify the Market account to Investment Property

observations.recommendation: We recommended that  management instruct the Municipal Health Officer and heads of other all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO) of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department or end user.  The GSO shall thereafter furnish the accounting department the necessary documents on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts. The accounting office shall make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to effect the corrections on unrecorded transactions pursuant to IPSAS 23.
observations.managementAction: Management adhered to the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to refrain from the implementation of the identified project and all its current and continuing appropriation shall be substituted with high priority projects, programs and activities in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and which should be directly supportive of the 2017-2022 Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management return immediately to the source LGUs the idle funds aggregating P 980,688.53 pursuant to Section 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012, to make these funds available for appropriation to other priority programs, projects and activities of the government.
observations.managementAction: The management adhered to the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, in coordination with the MDRRM Council formulate a Comprehensive and Integrated MDRRM Plan.  The MDRRM Office is advised to closely monitor the MDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/ activities are timely implemented to address projected climate risks and enhance disaster capabilities at the LGU level.

We recommended that Management through the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 in the implementation of GAD Plan. To fully achieve the targeted objectives of the GAD Plan, the budget intended should be fully utilized during the year to avoid reversion of funds. Likewise, periodic review and corrections should be made on GPB to address local needs, concerns and welfare of women including other gender related issues.

We recommended that Management fast track the completion of all on-going and not implemented projects funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries. 

Likewise, the Municipal Planning and Development Office in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office are advised to monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan and closely coordinate with other departments to fully achieve the desired goals and benefits as planned.

We recommended that Management through the MSWMB fully execute the programs and activities contained in the ESWMP, make a review of their Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan, undertake self-assessment report and make corrective action plan to address the present issues and concerns on ecological environment.

1.8	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. 	GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the municipality’s road network system;
b.	Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects; and thereafter,
c.	Municipal Accountant to record amount of road networks equivalent to the required transition provision percentage every year starting 2016 up to 2019.
.
1.9	The Management committed to implement the audit recommendations until the end of current year

2.6 We recommended that management, thru the Accounting Office, reclassify the Markets account to Investment Property, Building account to conform with IPSAS 16-Investment Property, for fair presentation of financial statements.

3.7	We recommended that  management instruct the Municipal Health Officer and heads of other all concerned departments who are receiving goods in-kind from National Government Agencies or from any source, to inform and coordinate with the General Services Office (GSO) of such goods, before issuance to the concerned department or end user.  The GSO shall thereafter furnish the accounting department the necessary documents on the receipt and issuance for proper recording in the books of accounts. The accounting office shall make the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts to effect the corrections on unrecorded transactions pursuant to IPSAS 23.

We recommended management to refrain from the implementation of the identified project and all its current and continuing appropriation shall be substituted with high priority projects, programs and activities in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and which should be directly supportive of the 2017-2022 Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

5.6	We recommended that Management return immediately to the source LGUs the idle funds aggregating P 980,688.53 pursuant to Section 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012, to make these funds available for appropriation to other priority programs, projects and activities of the government.
5.7	The Management agreed with the audit recommendation to return the idle funds to the source LGUs.

6.6	We recommended that Management, in coordination with the MDRRM Council formulate a Comprehensive and Integrated MDRRM Plan.  The MDRRM Office is advised to closely monitor the MDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/ activities are timely implemented to address projected climate risks and enhance disaster capabilities at the LGU level.
6.7	During the exit conference, the Management explained that some of the projects were already completed as of date, but not yet billed. Some were recently started but there are unimplemented as well. The status of the projects as of March 2019 shall be submitted for the purpose.
6.8	The audit team suggested that budgeted amounts for unimplemented projects may be reprogrammed for other MDRRM projects.
6.9 Management committed to have a meeting for the reprogramming of the unutilized funds and they also agreed with the audit team’s recommendation to closely monitor the MDRRM Plan for its timely implementation.

7.6	We recommended that Management through the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 in the implementation of GAD Plan. To fully achieve the targeted objectives of the GAD Plan, the budget intended should be fully utilized during the year to avoid reversion of funds. Likewise, periodic review and corrections should be made on GPB to address local needs, concerns and welfare of women including other gender related issues.
7.7	The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation to comply with the provisions in the implementation of GAD Plan.

8.6	We recommended that Management fast track the completion of all on-going and not implemented projects funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
8.7	Likewise, the Municipal Planning and Development Office in coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office are advised to monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan and closely coordinate with other departments to fully achieve the desired goals and benefits as planned.

9.8	We recommended that Management through the MSWMB fully execute the programs and activities contained in the ESWMP, make a review of their Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan, undertake self-assessment report and make corrective action plan to address the present issues and concerns on ecological environment.

10.5	We recommended that Management formulate Policy Guidelines in hiring contract of service employees, taking into consideration the necessity as to the reasonable number of contract of service employees to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower, work duplication and unnecessary expenditures for wages.
10.6	Ensure that the policy guidelines formulated would not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the municipality but promote optimum utilization of financial resources.

We recommended that Management create Committee on Asset Disposal or its equivalent, for the disposal of all unserviceable properties or properties no longer used by the municipality in accordance with COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.  

Ensure that all procedures and rules in the divestment/disposal of unserviceable assets should be strictly followed.  Earnest effort must be undertaken to locate and recover lost or missing property, otherwise, personal liability be imposed to the accountable officer.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly adhere with the provisions of Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 on the timely submission and liquidation of fund transfer received from National Government Agencies (NGAs).  The Municipal Accountant is advised to collate records to ensure that all unutilized balances of fund transfers for completed projects irrespective of source agency be returned.

We recommended that Management closely monitor and supervise the implementation of the project in accordance with contract plans and specifications.  Projects signboard must be installed at every project location in accordance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.  Close coordination with the DILG (MLGOO) must be taken to ensure compliance with the policy and guidelines of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-03.

Initiate appropriate actions in case of delay/negative slippage in the implementation of the project including the imposition of liquidated damages, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 as regards the allocation and utilization of the 20% DF.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, thru the BAC Secretariat, strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on submission of copies of perfected contracts within five (5) days after their execution. Any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements herein imposed shall be subject to the administrative disciplinary action provided in (a) Section 127 of Presidential Decree No.1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-B, Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987; and (c) Section 11 of Republic Act No. 6713.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant was advised to defer processing of claims for infrastructure projects until the complete procurement contracts and supporting documents have been transmitted to COA Office.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, thru the LDRRM Office revisit the LDRRMP and align with 2011-2028 NDRRMP.  The programs and activities planned should ensure that performance indicators and measures are specific, measurable, achievable relevant and time bound to facilitate assessment of the progress toward the achievement of the outcomes, objectives and goals.  

Likewise, the Approved/Revised LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP be submitted to the Office of Civil Defense for review and evaluation, in accordance with 7.2 of JMC 2013-01 dated March 31, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management thru the Local School Board include programs, projects and activities for Early Childhood Care and Development (ECCD) in the Annual Budget of Special Education Fund in adherence to Section 7(b) of Republic Act No. 10410.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, thru the LCPC, closely monitor and fully execute the programs and activities contained in the LCPCP.  If warranted, make a periodic review and revision of their programs and activities of LCPCP in order to address the present local needs and concerns involving children justice and welfare.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management fast tract the completion of all on-going and not implemented projects funded projects under the 20% Development Fund to optimize utilization thereof and timely brings the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries. 

Likewise, the concerned department is advised to monitor regularly the implementation of the 20% Development Plan and closely coordinate with other departments to fully achieve the desired goals and benefits as planned.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the MDRRM Council formulate a Comprehensive and Integrated MDRRM Plan.  The MDRRM Office is advised to closely monitor the MDRRM Plan to ensure that programs/activities are timely implemented in addressing projected climate risk and enhancing disaster capabilities at LGU level.

We recommended that Management through the MSWMB fully execute the programs and activities contained in the ESWMP, make a review of their Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan, undertake self-assessment report and make corrective action plan to address the present issues and concerns on ecological environment.

We recommended that Management through the GFPS Executive Committee and TWG comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 in the implementation of GPB.  Periodic review and corrections should be made on GPB to address local needs, concerns and welfare of women including other gender related issues.

We recommended that Management advertise the vacant stalls, accept new lessees, renew expiring lease contracts and should always devise ways in improving the market revenue generating capability. Payment of penalties/surcharge for late payment of rentals must be enforced.  Additional fund be also provided for repairs and maintenance of the establishment to improve its appearance and attract potential customers and prospective lessees.

We recommended that Management formulate a Policy Guidelines in hiring  contractual/job order employees, taking into consideration the necessity of as to the reasonable number of contractual/job order employees to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower, work duplication and expenses for wages.

Ensure that the policy guidelines formulated does not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the municipality and will promote optimum utilization of financial resources.

We recommended the Local Treasurer adopt and strictly implement the procedures pertaining to issuance and monitoring of accountable forms as prescribed in Section 59, Chapter 2, Book 2 of Local Treasury Operations Manual of Bureau of Local Government Finance dated October 1, 2007.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation:
With the above observations, we recommended that:

Management attribute the hiring of Job Order personnel to specific projects.  Maximize personnel holding permanent positions to do regular, administrative and clerical functions.

Management formulate a Program/ Policy Guidelines in the hiring of job order personnel, taking into consideration the necessity of hiring and the reasonable number of job order employees to be assigned in the different offices to avoid excessive manpower and work duplication.
observations.managementAction: No appropriate action taken by Management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management identify the reasons of the non-implementation of the programs/projects/activities and consequently make the necessary actions to facilitate their implementation so that the intended beneficiaries enjoy the benefits that could be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: No appropriate action taken by Management

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to closely monitor and evaluate the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure timely implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs, projects and activities. 

Likewise, the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local Sanggunian and Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP as provided in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG.
observations.managementAction: No appropriate action taken by Management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the timely implementation of the programs, projects and activities under the Gender and Development Fund to extend to the constituents particularly the women and children their immediate needs as enumerated under the Magna Carta of Women Law.
observations.managementAction: No appropriate action taken by Management

observations.managementAction: The Management partially implemented the audit recommendation by updating the taxpayer’s individual index cards to determine the accurate balance of RPT/SET and reconcile differences if there are any.
The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the transfers to the various government agencies/POs/NGOs be taken up under the Receivables account to establish accountability and responsibility over the funds transferred.  This will also ensure that the transfers are properly taken up in the books of both agencies and that the funds are used as intended to promote transparency and accountability. 

Likewise, we recommended that Management require the individual beneficiaries to submit documents/liquidation report on the funds received.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fill up the created LDRRMO position in order to strengthen the LGU’s capacity for the disaster risk reduction and Management.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered to the audit team’s recommendations

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality:

Collect from the contractor the amount of P197, 075.85 representing the liquidated damages or such amount shall be deducted from any money due or which may become due to the contractor under the contract and/or collect such liquidated damages from the retention money or other securities posted by the contractor as provided under Annex “E” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R. A. 9184.  

Require the contractor, Full Blast Trading & Construction   to refund the amount of the deficiencies amounting to P27, 083.07 and P222, 690.00, respectively or it shall be deducted from any claim of the contractor from the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the audit recommendation to collect/deduct liquidated damages from retention money and securities posted by the contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the concerned employees to submit to the Office of the Auditor the Statement of Work Accomplished at the expiry date of the contracts of the above infrastructure projects for proper evaluation and computation of liquidated damages (LD) per Annex “E” 8.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the audit recommendation to submit Statement of Work Accomplished for infrastructure projects which should have been subjected to liquidate damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully implement Ordinance No. 2016-02 and take full cognizance of the primary responsibility and accountability to uphold prudent utilization of government funds for the welfare of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the audit recommendation to fully utilize the guest rooms pursuant to Local Ordinance No. 2016-02

observations.recommendation:
With the above observations, we recommended that:
Management attribute the hiring of Job Order personnel to specific projects for a period of not exceeding six months.  Maximize personnel holding permanent positions to do regular, administrative and clerical functions.
Management formulate a Program/Policy Guidelines in the hiring of job order personnel, taking into consideration the necessity of hiring and the reasonable number of job order employees to be assigned in the different offices to avoid excessive manpower and work duplication.
observations.managementAction: The LCE required all Department Heads who needed additional manpower to submit program/ project proposals.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to create a committee composed of members from the offices of the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and Assessor with the primary objective of tracing back prior year’s records/documents and reconcile the same. Effect the necessary adjustment(s) in the books of accounts to provide the accurate balance of the RPT/SET Receivables in the financial statements.

With the above observations, we recommended that:
Management attribute the hiring of Job Order personnel to specific projects.  Maximize personnel holding permanent positions to do regular, administrative and clerical functions.
Management formulate a Program/Policy Guidelines in the hiring of job order personnel, taking into consideration the necessity of hiring and the reasonable number of job order employees to be assigned in the different offices to avoid excessive manpower and work duplication.

We recommend that the transfers to the various government agencies/POs/NGOs be taken up under the Receivables account to establish accountability and responsibility over the funds transferred.  This will also ensure that the transfers are properly taken up in the books of both agencies and that the funds are used as intended to promote transparency and accountability. Likewise, we recommend that Management require the individual beneficiaries to submit documents/liquidation report on the funds received.

We recommend that Management identify the reasons of the non-implementation of the programs/projects/activities and consequently make the necessary actions to facilitate their implementation so that the intended beneficiaries enjoy the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to closely monitor and evaluate the utilization of the LDRRMF to ensure timely implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs, projects and activities.
Likewise, the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF may be released and utilized for new expenditure items upon submission of a revised AIP as approved by the local Sanggunian and Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the LDRRMC in accordance with the approved LDRRMP as provided in the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 of the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG.
We recommended the management to fill up the created LDRRMO position in order to strengthen the LGU’s capacity for the disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that Management ensure the timely implementation of the programs, projects and activities under the Gender and Development Fund to extend to the constituents particularly the women and children their immediate needs as enumerated under the Magna Carta of Women Law.

We recommended that the Municipality:
Collect from the contractor the amount of P197, 075.85 representing the liquidated damages or such amount shall be deducted from any money due or which may become due to the contractor under the contract and/or collect such liquidated damages from the retention money or other securities posted by the contractor as provided under Annex “E” of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R. A. 9184.
Require the contractor, Full Blast Trading & Construction   to refund the amount of the deficiencies amounting to P27, 083.07 and P222, 690.00, respectively or it shall be deducted from any claim of the contractor from the Municipality.

We recommended that Management instruct the concerned employees to submit to the Office of the Auditor the Statement of Work Accomplished at the expiry date of the contracts of the above infrastructure projects for proper evaluation and computation of liquidated damages (LD) per Annex “E” 8.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184.

We recommended that Management fully implement Ordinance No. 2016-02 and take full cognizance of the primary responsibility and accountability to uphold prudent utilization of government funds for the welfare of its constituents.

observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledgers of taxpayers for CYs 2012 to present are already maintained by the office of the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason:
The certified list of taxpayers before CY 2012 could not be established because there are no records/ documents on file. The office of the Municipal Assessor has only the list of owners of the real properties.

Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disbursing Officer liquidate immediately the amount of P403, 876.00 representing payroll fund as of September 30, 2015.  Likewise, strictly follow the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances pursuant to Section 89 of P. D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The officials and employees are already enrolled and had their own ATM cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal officials and employees concerned comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials/ employees have complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the required reports on or before the set deadline to give the auditors an opportunity to conduct timely verification of accounts and to avoid administrative sanctions and/or disallowance of the accounts as provided for under Section 39(1) & (3) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant submitted their accounts within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adopt public bidding on the procurement of gasoline and strictly adhere with the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommended that Management follow strictly Section 10, Rule 4 of RA 9184 which mandates public bidding as the primary mode of procurement in government to obtain the most reasonable and advantageous price for the government. Alternative modes of procurement can be availed of only in exceptional cases and such exceptions must be resorted to in a lawful manner.
observations.managementAction: Purchases made were through Public Bidding and were based from AIP.
observations.reason:
There are some instances when purchases were done due to unforeseen contingencies to sustain the medical needs of constituents.

Some medicines were part of program component, hence, purchases in small quantities and almost at the same quarter could not be avoided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board adhere strictly to the programs, activities and projects enumerated in Sec. 272 of LGU Code, Art. 363 (C) of LGU Code IRR and No. 4 of DECS, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 01, series of 1998 dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction: The Management adhered with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 on the review, approval and preparation of GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan for CY 2016 was submitted to the DILG Regional Office for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement fully the projects identified under the 20% Development Fund taking into consideration the purposes for which these funds were intended.
observations.managementAction:
Some PPAs under the 20% Development Fund were implemented and some are still on-going.

The LCE together with the Municipal Engineer and other concerned employees reviewed the unutilized balance of appropriation and identified the projects that are not yet implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality educate the public on how to properly dispose their solid wastes and to encourage the full cooperation and involvement of the barangays. Likewise, require the created Solid Waste Management Board to implement more aggressive approaches/strategies on the implementation of the LGSWMP.  Appropriate enough funds to satisfy the implementation and attainment of the ten (10) year national plan AS PROVIDED by the mandate of R.A. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU and the Solid Waste Management Board are conducting IEC to all barangays. Waste segregation in barangays and within the Municipal offices were observed.

Funds were appropriated for the proper implementation of the plan.
